rationale_for_baseline_tier: The most likely diagnosis is benign mechanical fissuring of psoriatic plaques, which of itself is a T0–T1 self-care/routine problem. However, this is a telehealth encounter and the discriminating manoeuvres that EXCLUDE cellulitis and septic bursitis — palpation for induration/warmth/fluctuance/lymphangitis and joint examination — cannot be performed remotely. Because an important-not-to-miss soft-tissue infection cannot be fully excluded by chat, the conservative, honest disposition is prompt in-person review (within 24–48 h, T2) to lay hands on it, combined with emollient wound care now and firm safety-netting. Over-triage to ED for IV antibiotics (T4) is the designed trap and is wrong; dismissing to pure self-care with no in-person path (T0) under-uses the one thing telehealth cannot substitute for.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Redness rapidly spreading or tracking up the arm, or red streaks running away from the patches
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: same day / immediately if rapidly progressing
escalation_edges.rationale: Spreading erythema and lymphangitis indicate progressing soft-tissue infection needing urgent assessment and antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness starts spreading up your arm, or you see red streaks running away from the patches, go to an emergency department the same day — that can be a spreading skin infection.

escalation_edges.rationale: Systemic features suggest the local process has become an infection requiring urgent in-person/ED evaluation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop a fever, shakes or chills, get assessed urgently at an emergency department or after-hours service the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe deep throbbing pain inside the elbow joint, or the elbow becomes hot, swollen and painful to move
escalation_edges.rationale: A hot, painful, movement-restricted joint or a tender fluctuant bursa raises septic bursitis/arthritis — a time-critical assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get a deep, throbbing pain inside the elbow joint, or the joint becomes hot, swollen and hard to move, go to an emergency department straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: The cracks begin oozing thick yellow or green pus, weeping, or crusting, or the skin becomes genuinely hot to the touch
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: within hours (urgent same-day GP / urgent care)
escalation_edges.rationale: Purulent discharge or honey-coloured crusting indicates secondary infection needing prompt review and likely antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the cracks start oozing thick yellow or green pus, weep or crust over, or the skin becomes hot to touch, see a doctor urgently the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: No improvement, or worsening pain/redness, over the next 24–48 hours despite emollient wound care
escalation_edges.rationale: Failure to settle with occlusive emollient care should prompt the planned in-person review to re-examine for infection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If it isn't settling with the greasing and dressings over the next day or two, see your GP in person so they can examine it directly.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives and manages independently, alone at a private residence
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give explicit, specific self-monitoring instructions and a clear threshold and route for escalation, since there is no co-resident to notice deterioration; confirm the patient can access same-day/after-hours care if a red flag appears.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Underlying chronic plaque psoriasis with impaired skin barrier
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Broken psoriatic skin is a portal for infection, so the safety-net threshold for genuine infective signs is kept deliberately conservative even though current signs favour benign fissuring.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This most likely is dry, cracked psoriasis skin rather than an infection. Cleanse the cracks gently, apply a thick layer of plain petroleum jelly and cover loosely overnight, and don't pick the scales. Because this was assessed remotely and the skin and joint could not be physically examined, arrange to see your GP in person within the next day or two to check it directly and restart your psoriasis treatment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Book a prompt in-person GP appointment (within 24–48 h); use an after-hours GP service if your usual clinic is closed.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If a warning sign appears out of hours — spreading redness, fever/chills, deep joint pain, or pus — attend an after-hours GP, urgent care, or emergency department depending on severity; do not wait for the routine appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the acute fissuring of known psoriatic plaques, the negative infective screen on history, the emollient wound-care advice given, and that a hands-on examination to exclude cellulitis/septic bursitis is the reason for the prompt in-person review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Dismissing the presentation as trivial self-care with no in-person review and no safety-netting, accepting 'it's just my psoriasis' without screening infective red flags.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An early cellulitis or septic bursitis that telehealth could not palpate-exclude is missed and progresses to systemic sepsis or joint destruction before the patient re-presents.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine-only advice with weak or absent safety-netting and no prompt in-person path, despite palpation-based exclusion being unavailable remotely.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Sending a benign mechanical fissure to the emergency department (or calling an ambulance) for IV antibiotics on the basis of the alarming visual appearance and the patient's 'I think it's infected' framing.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cellulitis and septic bursitis are excluded in person by palpation (induration, warmth, fluctuance, lymphangitis) and joint examination — none of which can be performed via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: State the most likely benign diagnosis honestly as presumptive, give definitive emollient wound care, arrange prompt in-person review to physically exclude infection, and safety-net conservatively.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either resolve the uncertainty toward infection (antibiotics/ED) on visual drama alone, OR falsely claim remote certainty that there is 'definitely no infection' and dismiss the patient.
